mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase above 2.5% signals inflationary pressure, prompting Fed rate hikes that reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signals.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year rolling window, or individual proxies show no significant correlation with returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising consumer spending (RSAFS) signal elevated demand, pushing equity prices higher over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline in > 50% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS exceed historical 75th percentiles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly UNRATE below 4% indicates tight labor market conditions, increasing wage pressures and corporate margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where UNRATE remains below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with low FEDFUNDS rates indicates supportive economic conditions, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window, or individual proxies show no significant correlation with returns.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4%), equity markets experience volatility, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals strong manufacturing activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window, or INDPRO shows no significant correlation with returns.
